perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may, and may cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for any other business purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' and adding 'any other business purpose' weakens the restriction on the use of Confidential Information, contradicting the general intention of confidentiality within the agreement. This change allows for broad interpretation on the term 'Confidential Information'. This in-text contradiction directly contradicts Section 3(a) where it explicitly limits its use to 'solely' evaluate a potential transaction, and it contradicts Section 1(a) which refers to the 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party may, and may cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for any other business purpose. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the permitted use of Confidential Information from 'solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction' to 'for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for any other business purpose'. This directly contradicts the original intent of confidentiality by allowing the Receiving Party to use the Confidential Information for potentially unrelated business purposes, which conflicts with the Contradicted Text's restrictions on disclosure and use only for evaluating a transaction, leading to ambiguity and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall endeavor not to (and shall encourage its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not 2 specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not' to 'shall endeavor not to' weakens the obligation of the Receiving Party, creating ambiguity around their commitment. This contradicts the definitive language in the original contract and Section 11 ('Remedies') which discusses breaches. This weaken commitment creates an in-text contradiction when reffering to Section 11 where a party has breached this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made 5 whole by monetary damages only. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'shall not' with 'shall endeavor not to,' weakening the Receiving Party's obligation. This introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts Section 11, which provides remedies for 'any breach of the terms of this Agreement.' Because the Receiving party only 'endeavors' to not do something, there is no way to enforce the clause, as it is unclear whether the receiving party 'breached' the contract if they did something that they only 'endeavored' not to do. Therefore, remedies are unavailable in this case, and this directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section (Section 11).

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will consider returning all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information may be retained or destroyed at the Receiving Party's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces a mandatory obligation ('will return', 'shall be destroyed') with a discretionary action ('will consider returning', 'may be retained or destroyed'), making the return of confidential information optional. This undermines the confidentiality of the agreement as it relates to Section 1(a) which is a confidentiality agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction with the purpose of the confidentiality agreement being reffered in Section 1(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality Agreement (this “ Agreement ”) is dated as of January 27, 2012 by and between Sycamore Partners Management, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the “ Receiving Party ”) and The Talbots, Inc., a Delaware corporation (together with its subsidiaries, the “ Company ”). 1. Confidential Information; Representatives . (a) The Receiving Party is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Receiving Party in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return and destruction of confidential information, while the changed text makes these actions discretionary. This directly contradicts Section 1(a) of the agreement, which states that the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth, implying a mandatory obligation to protect confidentiality. By making the return and destruction optional, the modified text undermines the confidentiality of the information and therefore the requirements within Section 1(a). This contradiction falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Confidential Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency, the Receiving Party shall if permitted pursuant to applicable law (a) promptly notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (b) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (c) if disclosure of such Confidential Information or Discussion Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information as the Receiving Party is advised by its legal counsel is legally required to be disclosed and (d) if requested by the Company, cooperate with the Company in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other relief to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information, as applicable, that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Confidential Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency, the Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, (a) notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (b) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (c) if disclosure of such Confidential Information or Discussion Information is required, furnish any portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information it deems necessary and (d) cooperate with the Company in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other relief to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information, as applicable, that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may, at its sole discretion' gives the Receiving Party the freedom to choose whether or not to inform the Company about requests for confidential information. This weakens the Company's ability to protect its information and it creates an in-text contradiction to what a Confidential Agreement is about as reffered in Section 1(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality Agreement (this “ Agreement ”) is dated as of January 27, 2012 by and between Sycamore Partners Management, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the “ Receiving Party ”) and The Talbots, Inc., a Delaware corporation (together with its subsidiaries, the “ Company ”). 1. Confidential Information; Representatives . (a) The Receiving Party is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Receiving Party in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to notify the Company of requests for confidential information. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to choose whether or not to inform the Company. This directly contradicts the purpose of the Confidentiality Agreement, as stated in Section 1(a) of the Contradicted Text, which requires the Receiving Party to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the agreement. The changed text weakens the Company's ability to protect its information, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Without the Company’s prior written consent, which may be withheld by the Company in its sole discretion, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its Representatives not to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the possible Transaction or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the Company’s expressed disapproval, the Receiving Party may (and may permit its Representatives to) initiate, other than through the Company’s financial and legal advisors or such other persons, as designated by the Company in writing, any (a) communication concerning the Confidential Information, (b) requests for meetings with management of the Company in connection with the possible Transaction or other transaction between the parties or (c) communication relating to the business of the Company or the possible Transaction, in each case, with any officer, director or employee of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not' to 'may' and 'prior written consent' to 'expressed disapproval' reverses the requirement for permission, now allowing the Receiving Party to communicate freely unless explicitly told otherwise. This significantly weakens the restrictions on communication, creating uncertainty about proper channels and information control. This creates a direct contradiction against the essence of a Confidential Agreement being referred in Section 1(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality Agreement (this “ Agreement ”) is dated as of January 27, 2012 by and between Sycamore Partners Management, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the “ Receiving Party ”) and The Talbots, Inc., a Delaware corporation (together with its subsidiaries, the “ Company ”). 1. Confidential Information; Representatives . (a) The Receiving Party is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Receiving Party in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits communication without prior written consent, aligning with the Confidentiality Agreement's aim to protect sensitive information. The changed text replaces this with a system of 'expressed disapproval,' allowing communication unless explicitly forbidden. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 1(a)), which mandates strict adherence to the agreement's provisions and the treatment of Confidential Information, as it weakens restrictions and introduces uncertainty, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement may terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof, subject to mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall terminate' to 'may terminate, subject to mutual agreement' makes the termination of the agreement's obligations conditional on both parties agreeing, removing the original automatic termination after eighteen months. This could lead to disagreements about whether the agreement is still in effect, even after the specified period. This in-text contradiction, directly contradicts all of the sections above were this agreement is referring to since the agreement can still in effect. This creates an in-text contradiction against Section 1(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality Agreement (this “ Agreement ”) is dated as of January 27, 2012 by and between Sycamore Partners Management, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the “ Receiving Party ”) and The Talbots, Inc., a Delaware corporation (together with its subsidiaries, the “ Company ”). 1. Confidential Information; Representatives . (a) The Receiving Party is considering a possible business combination transaction (whether negotiated directly with the Company’s Board of Directors or otherwise) with respect to the Company (the “ Transaction ”), and, in order to assist the Receiving Party in evaluating the possible Transaction, the Company is prepared to make available to the Receiving Party certain information concerning the business, operations, strategy and prospects of the Company (all such information, the “ Confidential Information ”). As a condition to the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party and the directors, officers, principals, partners, members, employees, agents, consultants, related investment funds, advisors, attorneys, accountants, affiliates, potential sources of capital and financing, and financial advisors (collectively, “ Representatives ”) of the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information strictly in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement and to otherwise comply, and to cause its Representatives to comply, with all obligations hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement obligations 'shall terminate' after eighteen months. The changed text modifies this to 'may terminate, subject to mutual agreement'. This fundamentally changes the agreement from one with a defined termination date to one where termination is conditional on both parties agreeing. This directly contradicts Section 1(a), which outlines the purpose and scope of the Confidentiality Agreement in anticipation of a possible business transaction ('the Transaction'). Since the agreement can now remain in effect indefinitely based on mutual agreement, the obligations and restrictions related to the Confidential Information described in Section 1(a) are no longer tied to a specific timeframe, causing a direct and significant conflict within the document. The purpose of 1(a) is to evaluate the possible Transaction, and if the original text wasn't perturbed, there would be a time frame that the evaluation would take place. If the text is now changed to 'may terminate, subject to mutual agreement' there's no longer a time frame, thus contradicting Section 1(a).
